reason: The prompt targets a clear, boundary-aware judgment (higher post-explosion LEL reduces but does not remove flammability; CO rise indicates incomplete oxidation), which gives decent discriminative potential against a common safety misinterpretation. However, as a SHORT_ANSWER item there are no explicit decoys/options to audit, so single-fault purity and stylistic neutrality cannot be evaluated and the scoring signal depends heavily on grader interpretation of open-ended responses. Tightening the evaluation rubric and adding structured response constraints (or converting to MC with engineered decoys) would materially improve diagnostic value and reduce ambiguity in how much detail is required for full credit.
